evals.name: enterprise-legaltech
failures: SCAMPER not formally applied (in techniques_not_yet_used despite being on B2B riffing checklist)

cross_cutting_observations.detail: All 3 consumer evals loaded consumer-naming.md, ran Five Senses Scan, used consumer riffing checklist (not B2B), and adapted compound vocabulary to consumer domains
All 3 enterprise evals loaded enterprise-naming.md, applied enterprise-specific tests (buyer committee, sales conversation fit, etc.), and used B2B riffing checklist
Streetwear eval correctly skipped B2B compound track per SKILL.md guidance. Hot sauce used food-appropriate compounds. Men's skincare used beauty-appropriate compounds.
All 6 evals passed all 4 hard gates (candidate count, category coverage, structural diversity, technique cycling). Average 182 candidates vs 60 minimum.

cross_cutting_observations.finding: Technique tracking YAML frontmatter consistently produced
cross_cutting_observations.detail: All 6 evals produced techniques_used and techniques_not_yet_used arrays in 01-candidates.md frontmatter, enabling technique accountability

cross_cutting_observations.finding: Minor gap: consumer evaluation techniques (shelf test, social-native) not always formally tracked
cross_cutting_observations.detail: Hot sauce eval missed shelf_test and social_native_testing in techniques_used even though brief mentioned Instagram handle. Men's skincare DID track both. Suggests these are sometimes done informally but not recorded.

cross_cutting_observations.finding: Minor gap: SCAMPER occasionally skipped in B2B riffing checklist
cross_cutting_observations.detail: Legaltech eval had SCAMPER in techniques_not_yet_used despite B2B checklist requiring it. Other B2B evals completed SCAMPER. May be a prioritization issue when technique count is already high (30+ techniques used).
